--- title: "6. Optional: Hierarchical Clustering" subtitle: "An Alternative to K-means for Survey Data" --- If you want a non-K-means alternative for the survey case, hierarchical clustering is often useful. `AgglomerativeClustering` does not require specifying a random state and can produce more stable solutions on small datasets. ::: {#cell-survey-hierarchical .cell execution_count=2} ::: {#survey-hierarchical .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
true_segment Quality Loyalists Trend Explorers Value Seekers
segment_hier
0 0 0 120
1 0 120 0
2 120 0 0
``` ::: ::: Compare the cross-tab here with the one from the [Survey page](survey.qmd) to see how closely the two methods agree on the latent segment recovery.
